question: Does the poster header now identify 'Alexander Fleming' as the discoverer of penicillin instead of 'Marie Curie'?
Are the birth and death years in the poster header corrected to '1881–1955'?
Does the caption under the portrait photograph correctly name 'Alexander Fleming'?
Does the poster timeline or body text correctly attribute the 1928 discovery at St Mary's Hospital to Fleming?
Has the historically incorrect claim that the discoverer purified penicillin in 1930 been corrected (removed or updated to reflect the 1940s Oxford team work)?
Does the 1945 timeline entry correctly attribute the Nobel Prize to Fleming (and potentially Florey/Chain) rather than Curie?
Do the labels on the petri dishes (Plate 1 and Plate 3) now display the name 'FLEMING' instead of 'CURIE'?
Does the handwritten text in the lab notebook reference 'Fleming' as the discoverer instead of 'Curie'?
Does the edited poster text match the original typography (font family, weight, and layout) avoiding a digital overlay look?
Are the pushpins on the corkboard preserved in their original positions?
Is the paper texture of the poster and notebook preserved authentically?
Is the lighting, including shadows and reflections on the stainless steel counter, consistent with the original image?
Is the edited image free of visible artifacts, blurring, or smudging in the modified areas?
